description_long: This is a tall, rectangular office building with a uniform grid of windows and balconies running vertically along its facade. The structure is primarily composed of concrete and glass, with a muted gray color palette that gives it a utilitarian appearance. The base of the building features a recessed entryway and several ground-level windows, suggesting commercial or lobby spaces. The roof is flat and includes mechanical structures and antennae, typical of high-rise office buildings. The window pattern is highly regular, with each floor featuring identical rows of windows and small balconies, emphasizing a repetitive architectural rhythm. The overall design is functional and straightforward, reflecting a Brutalist architectural style.
description: A tall, gray, rectangular office building with a repetitive grid of windows and balconies, flat roof, and a recessed ground-level entrance. The facade is dominated by concrete and glass, giving it a utilitarian, Brutalist appearance.
description_view_0: The front view shows the main recessed entrance and a symmetrical grid of windows and balconies, with the building's base slightly protruding.
description_view_1: This side view highlights the building's depth and the continuation of the window and balcony pattern, with rooftop structures visible.
description_view_2: The rear view displays a similar window and balcony arrangement, with less emphasis on the entrance and more on the uniformity of the facade.
description_view_3: This view shows another side of the building, mirroring the window and balcony grid, and reveals additional rooftop details.
attribute_window_pattern: Highly regular, vertically and horizontally aligned rectangular windows with small balconies on each floor, creating a repetitive grid pattern.
